subject: Overnight Incident Blotter — Sat 6/20 into Sun 6/21
body_clean: Watch summary, 1900 hrs Sat 6/20 to 0700 hrs Sun 6/21. Calls for service: 31. Notable: 0142 hrs — loud party complaint, 400 blk of 25th Ave, dispersed without citation. 0235 hrs — suspicious vehicle, lot behind 500 blk St. Charles Rd, gone on arrival. 0318 hrs — residential alarm, 1700 blk Frederick Ave, accidental (homeowner). 0510 hrs — single-vehicle crash, Mannheim Rd at Washington Blvd, no injuries, driver cited for failure to reduce speed. Two custodial arrests: one DUI (Eastern Ave), one outstanding warrant (Marshall Ave traffic stop). No shots-fired calls. Three units in service overnight. Quiet relative to last weekend.

subject: Overnight Incident Blotter — Sun 6/21 into Mon 6/22
body_clean: Watch summary, 1900 hrs Sun 6/21 to 0700 hrs Mon 6/22. Calls for service: 24. Notable: 2211 hrs — domestic disturbance, 2200 blk Bohland Ave, verbal only, no arrest, referral provided. 0040 hrs — catalytic converter saw heard, 1000 blk 25th Ave; offenders fled in dark sedan before units arrived (see case B26-04417). 0355 hrs — fire alarm assist, commercial, 600 blk St. Charles Rd, false trip. 0612 hrs — recovered stolen vehicle, 3300 blk Washington Blvd, plate matched a Cook County BOLO. One custodial arrest: retail theft hold transferred from Saturday. Three units in service. A patrol emphasis was added to the 1000 blk 25th Ave for the converter activity.

subject: Offense Report B26-04392 — Retail Theft, 200 Mannheim Rd
body_clean: Case B26-04392. Offense: Retail Theft (720 ILCS 5/16-25). Location: pharmacy, 245 Mannheim Rd. Reported 6/16 at 1452 hrs. A loss-prevention employee detained a 34-year-old male who concealed $214 in over-the-counter medication and razor blades and exited past the last point of sale. Subject held for officers; merchandise recovered and returned to shelf-sellable condition. Disposition: arrest, released on I-bond with a 7/14 first appearance at the Maywood courthouse. Subject had two prior retail-theft contacts in 2025. Store provided surveillance footage; copy logged into evidence. Forwarding to the State's Attorney for review.

subject: Offense Report B26-04405 — Motor Vehicle Theft, 1500 blk 50th Ave
body_clean: Case B26-04405. Offense: Motor Vehicle Theft (720 ILCS 5/4-103). Location: 1522 50th Ave (driveway). Occurred overnight 6/16-6/17, reported 6/17 at 0625 hrs. A 2017 Kia Sportage taken from the driveway; owner reports the spare key may have been left in the center console. Estimated value $14,000. Vehicle entered into LEADS/NCIC as stolen and a regional BOLO issued. No glass found at the scene, consistent with the relay/USB-cable method seen in other Kia/Hyundai thefts. Disposition: open / active. Owner provided with theft-report packet for insurance and advised on the manufacturer anti-theft software update. Plate and VIN flagged with the toll-camera network.

subject: Offense Report B26-04417 — Catalytic Converter Theft, 1000 blk 25th Ave
body_clean: Case B26-04417. Offense: Theft / Criminal Damage to Vehicle (720 ILCS 5/16-1, 5/21-1). Location: 1031 25th Ave (street parking). Occurred overnight 6/20-6/21, reported 6/21 at 0738 hrs by the owner of a 2009 Honda Element. Catalytic converter cut from the exhaust; estimated repair $1,650. A neighbor's doorbell camera captured two subjects and a dark four-door sedan, partial plate logged. No suspects in custody. Disposition: open / active. This is the third converter theft on the 25th Ave corridor since May. Patrol emphasis requested for overnight hours. Victim advised to consider an etched-VIN or anti-theft shield.

subject: Offense Report B26-04428 — Residential Burglary, 1700 blk Frederick Ave
body_clean: Case B26-04428. Offense: Residential Burglary (720 ILCS 5/19-3). Location: 1719 Frederick Ave. Occurred between 6/18 0800 and 1730 hrs while occupants were at work, reported same day at 1751 hrs. Entry through a forced rear kitchen window; jewelry and a laptop taken, est. loss $2,400. No alarm system. Evidence tech processed the point of entry and lifted two latent prints, submitted to the lab. Canvass of the block produced one neighbor who saw an unfamiliar white van mid-afternoon. Disposition: open / active, assigned to investigations. Victim referred to the village's free home-security survey program.

subject: Offense Report B26-04443 — Criminal Damage to Property, Lincoln School playground
body_clean: Case B26-04443. Offense: Criminal Damage to Property (720 ILCS 5/21-1). Location: Lincoln Elementary playground, 540 Bohland Ave. Occurred overnight 6/18-6/19, reported 6/19 at 0710 hrs by district maintenance. Graffiti tagged on the gym wall and slide, plus a damaged drinking fountain; est. repair and cleanup $1,300. Photos taken and tags documented for comparison with prior incidents. No witnesses; park district camera was offline. Disposition: open / active. Forwarding to school resource officer and the parks coordinator for camera repair and a cleanup work order. Patrol asked to add evening checks of the playground through the summer.

subject: Offense Report B26-04451 — Financial Fraud (Gift-Card Scam), 1000 blk Marshall Ave
body_clean: Case B26-04451. Offense: Financial Identity Theft / Deceptive Practice (720 ILCS 5/16-30). Location: residence, 1044 Marshall Ave. Reported 6/19 at 1120 hrs. A 71-year-old resident received a call from someone posing as a utility-company representative threatening same-day disconnection unless a balance was paid via prepaid gift cards. Victim purchased and read off six cards totaling $1,200 before a family member intervened. Card numbers documented; one card was frozen with a partial recovery pending. Disposition: open / referred to financial-crimes detective and the FTC complaint portal. PSA on the gift-card scam pattern recommended for the senior newsletter. Victim provided fraud-affidavit packet.

subject: Heads-up — converter thefts on 25th Ave corridor (case B26-04417)
body_clean: Mayor, a quick heads-up before you hear it from residents. We took a third catalytic converter theft on the 25th Ave corridor overnight Saturday into Sunday (B26-04417), bringing us to three since May, all clustered in the 900-1100 blocks. We have partial plate and doorbell video on the most recent one and we're working it with neighboring departments, since the pattern matches a regional crew hitting older Hondas. Effective tonight I've added a directed overnight patrol on that stretch and we'll be pushing a community alert tomorrow. No injuries, property only. I'll keep you posted if we make an arrest.

subject: Heads-up — converter thefts on 25th Ave corridor (case B26-04417)
body_clean: Chief, thank you for the early word — I'd rather hear it from you first. The directed patrol and the community alert are exactly right. A few residents on 25th have already emailed me, so I'll respond letting them know enforcement is stepped up and point them to the alert once it goes out. Can you make sure the alert includes the etched-VIN and shield options and the non-emergency number to report anything suspicious overnight? And please loop me the moment there's an arrest so I can recognize the work publicly. Appreciate you and the overnight crew.

subject: Community Safety Alert — Catalytic converter thefts & summer prevention tips
body_clean: For Monday's distribution. COMMUNITY SAFETY ALERT: The Bellwood Police Department is investigating a series of catalytic converter thefts on the 25th Ave corridor (900-1100 blocks), most recently overnight 6/20-6/21 (case B26-04417). Older Honda and Toyota models are being targeted overnight. We have added directed overnight patrols to the area. Residents can reduce risk by parking in a garage when possible, installing a converter shield, having the VIN etched at our free June 28 clinic (10 a.m.-1 p.m., Public Works yard), and reporting any cutting/grinding sounds immediately to non-emergency dispatch at 708-555-0112 (call 911 if a crime is in progress). Please share with neighbors and watch out for one another this summer.
